title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 217.220 - Death sentence commutation, admittance to correctional center
text:
217.220.  Death sentence commutation, admittance to correctional center. — The director shall at all times receive into the department, on order of the governor, any person convicted of any crime punishable with death.  If the governor commutes the sentence to imprisonment either for life or for a term of years in a correctional center, as provided by law, the offender shall be confined according to the terms of the commutation.
(L. 1982 H.B. 1196 § 55, A.L. 1989 H.B. 408, A.L. 1995 H.B. 424)
